judge_reason: One side quotes 'EX-112' as a stamped label at the top right, while the other describes it as a 'handwritten label' and adds 'Mr. Hyland Report' as typed text, creating contradictory and hallucinated descriptions of the same page content.
original_image_tags: The page is a faded, yellowed document with visible text from the other side of the paper bleeding through. The top and bottom edges are torn and uneven. The primary visible text consists of several rubber-stamped "RECEIVED" timestamps from the FBI in February 1972. At the top right, a stamped label reads "EX-112". The rest of the page shows faint, mirrored text from the reverse side, including the words "keep these reports" and "I regard these...".
gpt_mini_desc: *Image: Faint scanned blank-looking typed memorandum page with multiple reversed/mirrored rubber-stamp markings, including “FBI,” “RECEIVED IN F.B.I.,” and several date stamps; three punch holes along the top edge.*
